conclusion: Common carp and related cyprinids (goldfish, Prussian carp) can colonize isolated urban stormwater ponds via waterfowl gut dispersal — a peer-reviewed mechanism (Lovas-Kiss et al. 2020, PNAS). High waterfowl activity near isolated ponds increases colonization probability. Worth sampling with microfishing gear even without hydrological connectivity.
source_detail: Lovas-Kiss et al. 2020 PNAS 117(27):15397-15399; Biological Invasions 2025 stormwater pond dispersal
